primary_concern: Sepsis-3 criteria met with concurrent AKI Stage 3 and coagulopathy
clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) combined with leukocytosis (14.2 K/uL) and hemoglobin 9.8 g/dL meet Sepsis-3 criteria for suspected infection plus acute organ dysfunction. Platelets have progressively declined to 88 K/uL (Jan 12 09:00) with SOFA coagulation scoring threshold crossed. Concurrent AKI progression from Stage 1 (cr 2.1 mg/dL Jan 11) to Stage 3 (cr 8.9 mg/dL Jan 12) is documented despite outlier-excluded creatinine spike.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L + WBC >12 K/uL + creatinine elevation meets Sepsis-3 definition (suspected infection + SOFA increase ≥2)
prioritized_risks.threshold: Suspected infection plus acute SOFA increase ≥2

prioritized_risks.explanation: Creatinine rise ≥1.5× baseline (Stage 3) with rapid progression to 8.9 mg/dL
Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA score 3 threshold with spontaneous bleeding risk
Lactate >2 mmol/L present but vasopressor requirements unknown

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per sepsis protocol
Order blood cultures and infection source workup
Start crystalloid resuscitation (30 mL/kg) per Sepsis-3 bundle
Monitor lactate clearance and repeat platelet counts q4h
Consult nephrology for AKI Stage 3 management
doctor_handoff: Review for source control of infection, assess need for vasopressors if hypotension develops, and evaluate for thrombocytopenia etiology. Monitor urine output and renal function trends.

disease_progression.observation: Initial derangements: WBC 14.2 K/uL, Hgb 9.8 g/dL, Bilirubin 1.4 mg/dL
AKI Stage 1 (Cr 2.1 mg/dL) with platelets 95 K/uL
AKI Stage 3 (Cr 8.9 mg/dL) with platelets 88 K/uL

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
